title: agoseris cuspidata
intro: Agoseris cuspidata, also known as prickly agoseris, is a perennial plant species native to the western regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain inulin and other saponins, and have been used to treat various health issues. Prickly agoseris has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can lead to several health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and its roots can be dried and made into teas, capsules, or powders for consumption.
